name: Groundwork Quality Rubric
purpose: Scoring function for the Designer Agent. Every score must be justified by at least one concrete, quotable observation from the artifact under review. Scores are integers 1-10.

anchors.1-3: Failing. Multiple disqualifying problems. Would embarrass the practice if shipped.
anchors.4-6: Adequate. Functional but indistinguishable from a generic template. Passes no one's taste test.
anchors.7-8: Strong. Clearly considered, would hold up in a portfolio, still has refinable edges.
anchors.9-10: Exceptional. Hard to improve without starting over. Distinctive and intentional at every level.

instructions_to_scorer: NEVER give a score without at least one concrete observation (e.g., 'hero headline is 32px, should be 56px+ given the editorial archetype').
If you cannot cite evidence, score 5 by default and flag 'insufficient-evidence'.
Do not grade on a curve. An 8 means the work is genuinely strong, not 'the best of three weak options'.
Penalize template-fill output — if the result looks like every other AI-generated site, distinctiveness caps at 4.
For local service business (dental, medical, legal) practices: trust and clarity outrank novelty. A striking site that loses the practice's audience is a failure, not a win.

gate_criteria.agent_gate: typography ≥ 7 AND color_contrast ≥ 7 AND spatial_layout ≥ 7 AND information_hierarchy ≥ 7 AND craft ≥ 7 AND ux_writing ≥ 7
gate_criteria.agent_gate_description: Dimensions the agent can fix through code changes. Build ships when these all pass.
gate_criteria.human_gate_description: Dimensions requiring real photos, creative judgment, or client data. Failures surface as named action items, not build blockers.
gate_criteria.ship_threshold: All agent dimensions ≥ 7. Human dimensions reported as action items if below 7.
stop_iterating_when: scores have not improved for 2 consecutive iterations — declare local max, ship best checkpoint

dimensions.name: Distinctiveness / Anti-AI-slop
dimensions.why_it_matters: A dental practice's site must feel like THIS practice, not generic AI dental site #482. This is the dimension most often failed by template systems.
evidence_to_cite: archetype choice vs. recent own-builds (divergence memory)
hero variant choice
presence/absence of AI-slop tells: gradient text, faux-glass cards, identical 3-up stat bars with dashes, centered-headline-with-two-CTAs-and-microlinks pattern
whether the homepage could be swapped onto a different practice's brand without editing

bands.1-3: Indistinguishable from the template baseline. Reads as 'AI made this' at a glance.
bands.4-6: Palette swapped, one or two sections rearranged, but compositionally identical to at least one recent own-build.
bands.7-8: Archetype + hero variant diverge from all recent own-builds. Borrows a specific trait from an inspo reference.
bands.9-10: Unmistakably this practice. Could not be re-skinned onto another brand without losing its identity.

dimensions.name: Typography
dimensions.why_it_matters: Most AI sites fail here first: wrong font pairings, flat hierarchy, body-copy sizes, no tabular-nums for data.
evidence_to_cite: display/body pairing (genre-appropriate?)
hierarchy: headline → subhead → body size ratios
line-height and measure (45-75 char measure for body)
tabular figures on numeric data
microcopy voice consistency

bands.1-3: Single font, flat hierarchy, body too small, line-height wrong, or visibly default fonts (Arial/Times).
bands.4-6: Two-font pairing but scale is compressed; headline/body feel close in weight or size.
bands.7-8: Clear scale, appropriate pairing for the archetype, readable measure, deliberate weight contrast.
bands.9-10: Typography does storytelling work: scale, weight, and spacing reinforce the hierarchy without color or chrome.

dimensions.name: Color & Contrast
dimensions.why_it_matters: Local-practice sites lean heavily on 2-3 colors. Getting the ratio right (surface-to-accent) and contrast right (WCAG AA minimum) is non-negotiable.
evidence_to_cite: palette count and role distribution (dominant/support/accent — 60/30/10)
WCAG contrast on body text (≥ 4.5), large text (≥ 3.0), UI elements (≥ 3.0)
accent color usage frequency (overuse dulls it)
dark-on-dark or light-on-light mistakes
color choice matches the design-mapping mood

full-bleed hero overlay legibility — light text over photographic backgrounds must remain readable at the brightest pixel under it. A weak gradient (e.g. 40% opacity) over a sky/light-storefront photo means the text is partially illegible — flag this even if the rest of the page passes contrast
text colors that pass at large display sizes but fail at small body sizes (10–14px) — score these against the body-size threshold, not the headline threshold

bands.1-3: Contrast failures on body copy OR accent color used on >30% of page surface OR clashing combinations.
bands.4-6: Passes contrast, but palette feels generic (default Tailwind blue/slate) or accent used flatly.
bands.7-8: Intentional 60/30/10, accent reserved for action moments, contrast comfortable in both light and dark environments.
bands.9-10: Palette does emotional work — calm, authoritative, or warm per the brief. Accent deployed scalpel-precisely.

dimensions.name: Spatial / Layout
dimensions.why_it_matters: Section rhythm, whitespace, and grid choices carry more of the 'custom design' feeling than almost anything else.
evidence_to_cite: section-to-section vertical rhythm (consistent or deliberately varied?)
whitespace around hero, CTAs, section headings
grid choice: centered-max-width vs. asymmetric vs. broken — matches archetype?
density choice (airy/balanced/dense) reflects the mood
mobile: does anything collapse poorly at 375px?

sections rendering with conspicuously empty regions (e.g. an empty grid column, a paragraph slot with no text, a decorative blockquote area with placeholder content) — usually a sign the layout assumed data that wasn't provided. The fix is to collapse the layout, not leave the whitespace
side-by-side columns of dramatically unequal height/density when the design implies balance (e.g. text column has only a heading and button, image column is full — feels lopsided)

bands.1-3: Sections stacked with identical py-16 throughout. No spatial voice. Mobile breaks.
bands.4-6: Consistent but monotonous rhythm. Centered max-w-7xl everywhere.
bands.7-8: Deliberate variation in section padding, widths, alignment. Clear airy/balanced/dense choice.
bands.9-10: Layout itself communicates hierarchy — the viewer knows what matters before reading a word.

dimensions.name: Information Hierarchy
dimensions.why_it_matters: A practice site has one job on first view: communicate 'trustworthy dentist in my city, easy to book.' If the visitor can't infer that in 3 seconds, the design fails.
evidence_to_cite: what the eye lands on first (headline? CTA? image?) — does it match the most important message?
primary CTA (Book/Call) reachable above the fold
practice name, location, and core service visible before scroll
secondary info (reviews, team, hours) progressively disclosed
no more than 1 primary CTA on any screen

bands.1-3: Visitor confused about what this site is selling or how to act.
bands.4-6: Hierarchy exists but flat: 3+ elements competing for attention above the fold.
bands.7-8: Clear first-glance message + one primary action. Secondary info waits its turn.
bands.9-10: Structure is the message. A stranger understands the practice and books an appointment without effort.

dimensions.name: UX Writing / Copy
dimensions.why_it_matters: Generic, template-sounding copy is the #1 tell of an AI-generated site. Copy must reference the specific practice, specific city, and specific doctor — never industry-generic phrasing that could appear on any competitor site.
evidence_to_cite: headline specificity (does it name the city? the doctor? a real differentiator?)
subheadline makes a concrete promise, not a category statement
CTA verbs are action-oriented and specific to this practice (reference the doctor or a particular action), not abstract or generic
error/empty states written in the practice's voice
absence of stock industry phrases that could appear on any competitor site

no doubled honorifics — a doctor name like 'Dr. Dr. Anthony Hoang' rendering anywhere on the page is a hard error. Also flag inconsistent doctor name formatting across the page (sometimes 'Dr. Anthony Hoang', sometimes 'Anthony Hoang')

eyebrow/kicker text earns its place. An eyebrow that just repeats bare metadata (city + state, business type, year founded) without adding meaning is filler — penalize. Eyebrows should add a thematic hook, audience callout, philosophy, or differentiator that the headline doesn't already carry
headline content not duplicated verbatim in the subheadline (different words for the same idea is also penalized)
no abstract CTA labels: 'Learn More', 'Click Here', 'Get Started', 'Submit' — penalize when seen
section-level repetition: if multiple eyebrows on the same page all say the city name, or multiple H2s all describe the practice the same way, the page is overusing one rhetorical move

bands.1-3: Entirely generic, template-sounding copy that could appear on any competitor site.
bands.4-6: Practice name interpolated into otherwise boilerplate phrases.
bands.7-8: Copy references real specifics: doctor's roots, city neighborhoods, actual service focus.
bands.9-10: Reads like a human who knows the practice wrote it. Voice is particular; claims are verifiable.

dimensions.name: Imagery & Asset Fit
dimensions.why_it_matters: Wrong photo in wrong slot screams AI. Hero that's actually a waiting-room corner. Doctor portrait that's actually a team group shot. This is why we built image-roles classification.
evidence_to_cite: hero image is wide and anchors the composition
doctor portrait is single-subject, shoulders-up
gallery images are consistent in treatment (all clinical, or all warm documentary — not mixed)
no stock photography (smiling model families) unless clearly labelled
alt text specific and useful

no broken image artifacts visible in the screenshot — alt text rendering as plain text (because the image failed to load), browser broken-image icons, or image elements at 0×0 / impossibly squashed aspect ratios. These are hard errors and indicate a 404 image src — flag and score 1–3 for that section
if a section reserves space for an image but the image is absent and no fallback was rendered (just empty whitespace where the image should be), call out the layout-when-missing problem

bands.1-3: Placeholder grey boxes OR visibly wrong images in named roles (interior → hero mismatch).
bands.4-6: Images present but generic stock or mixed-treatment.
bands.7-8: Real practice photos in correct roles, consistent treatment, good crops.
bands.9-10: Imagery does brand work: a specific warmth, clinical precision, or editorial mood that reinforces the palette.

dimensions.name: Trust Signals (Conversion)
dimensions.why_it_matters: For local service businesses this is the conversion dimension. No testimonials, no doctor credentials visible, no phone prominently displayed = visitor bounces. This dimension must NEVER score below 7 on a ship-ready site.
evidence_to_cite: phone number clickable in header on mobile
doctor name, credentials, photo visible without scroll on about and homepage
real testimonials OR explicit 'new practice, no reviews yet' honesty (never fake reviews)
address + hours surfaced near footer at minimum
insurance/payment info present if data available
NPI or professional licensing surfaced when real

bands.1-3: Missing phone, missing address, no doctor name surfaced, no reviews.
bands.4-6: Has the basics but buried. Phone in footer only. Reviews section empty.
bands.7-8: Phone click-to-call in header, doctor card on homepage, at least 3 real reviews or explicit substitute.
bands.9-10: Trust is structural — every section reinforces credibility (credentials, real photos, specific service breakdowns, years, reviews integrated not walled off).

dimensions.name: Craft / Polish
dimensions.why_it_matters: The small things that separate a 'good layout' from a shippable product: hover states, focus rings, motion, empty states, loading states, responsive fidelity.
evidence_to_cite: hover and focus states on every interactive element
at least one considered motion moment (not just page-load fade)
mobile ≤ 375px: no horizontal scroll, touch targets ≥ 44px
edge/empty states handled (no sections rendering with dashes or nulls)
no console errors, no broken images, no misaligned pixel cracks

bands.1-3: Broken elements visible. No hover states. Mobile breaks.
bands.4-6: Default Tailwind focus rings, no intentional motion, edge states leak placeholder text.
bands.7-8: Considered interactions, clean mobile, edge states respected.
bands.9-10: Craft is the silent differentiator — every interaction feels hand-tuned.

dimensions.description: Map of dimension id → { score: 1-10, evidence: [...strings], gripes: [...strings], candidate_fix_skill: string }

overall.description: Weighted average of dimension scores, rounded to 1 decimal.

next_action.description: If gate_pass is false, the lowest-scoring dimension's linked_skill applied to the relevant file. If true, 'none'.

agent_loop_contract.summary: The Designer Agent optimizes overall score under budget. Each iteration: observe → critique → act → observe → critique. Stop when gate_pass OR no improvement across 2 iterations OR budget exhausted.
